content:
===BEGIN===
Imagine you have a magic piggy bank that grows your money all by itself! This magic is called compound interest. Here’s how it works: when you put money in the piggy bank, it doesn’t just sit there. It earns extra money, called interest. Then, the next time, the piggy bank earns interest not only on the money you put in but also on the extra money it already made. It’s like a snowball rolling down a hill, getting bigger and bigger.

For example, if you save $10 and the piggy bank gives you 10% interest each year, after one year, you have $11. The next year, you get 10% interest on $11, which is $1.10, so now you have $12.10. Over many years, this keeps happening, and your money grows faster and faster! That’s why it’s great to start saving early and let compound interest work its magic!
